rm-greeting.speaker: registration-manager
rm-greeting.text: Welcome to DevConnect 2026! I'm Marlene, Registration Manager. Are you lost, or are you actually supposed to be here?

choices.text: I lost my badge. Can you reprint it?
Just browsing. Which talks are good?
Right, I should be around. Thanks!

rm-badge.speaker: registration-manager
rm-badge.text: We got a report of a badge found in the Expo Hall near the grabber machine. Want me to send Security? Or would you rather... retrieve it yourself?

choices.text: I'll go get it myself. Thanks!
Send Security, I'm too important to run errands.
Never mind, I'll figure it out.

rm-badge-go.speaker: registration-manager
rm-badge-go.text: Great. The gates are opening in five. Also, I've got your registration info — your talk proposal was borderline. Maybe try networking more?

choices.text: Noted. I'll make friends fast.

rm-badge-sec.speaker: registration-manager
rm-badge-sec.text: Wow. Alright, I'll note your preference for VIP service. 'Too important' it is. Sorry, j/k, I actually can't do that.

choices.text: Just kidding. I'll go get it myself.

rm-talks.speaker: registration-manager
rm-talks.text: Honestly? Everything sounds the same this year. 'AI-powered blockchain for serverless puppies.' Go figure. The only unique talk got cancelled.

choices.text: Cancelled? What happened?
Thanks, Marlene. You rule.

rm-cancelled.speaker: registration-manager
rm-cancelled.text: The keynote speaker 'had a last-minute family emergency.' Also, he left a very dramatic note in the green room. The organizer is definitely stressed.

choices.text: This sounds like a problem I could solve.
Interesting... and tragic.

rm-cancelled-interest.speaker: registration-manager
rm-cancelled-interest.text: If you're serious, talk to the Conference Organizer. They're usually near the Theater stage. And Marlene appreciates ambition. (I got you. That was me.)

choices.text: Thanks! I'll head there now.

vr-greeting.speaker: vendor-representative
vr-greeting.text: Hi! Welcome to CloudNine Systems! Have you tried our new AI-Powered Cloud Toaster? Half the toast, twice the machine learning!

choices.text: Looks like your demo is crashing.
Can I help with anything?
No thanks, just looking.

vr-greeting-quest-active.speaker: vendor-representative
vr-greeting-quest-active.text: Oh, you're back! My demo is STILL broken. The debug console is showing... colors. That's bad. Colors shouldn't be there.

choices.text: I'll check it out.
Do you have a USB with the fix?

vr-help.speaker: vendor-representative
vr-help.text: Actually, yes. My demo kiosk keeps blue-screening right before investors walk by. The IT tech said the deployment USB might be corrupted. Can you grab a replacement from the server room?

choices.text: Sure, I'll head to the server room.
That sounds like a YOU problem.

vr-help-accept.speaker: vendor-representative
vr-help-accept.text: You're a lifesaver! The IT tech is usually near the Theater or Coffee bar. And if this works, I'll give you our premium swag — a coupon for a free t-shirt!

choices.text: A t-shirt? I've never been more motivated.

vr-crash.speaker: vendor-representative
vr-crash.text: Yes! It's been crashing since this morning. Every time someone important looks at it, it blue-screens. I think it's cursed.

choices.text: Do you have a USB with the fix?
I'll go talk to IT about it.

vr-demo-no-usb.speaker: vendor-representative
vr-demo-no-usb.text: I used the original USB as a drink coaster. Don't ask. The IT guy in the server room might have a replacement. Or you could just plug in your own USB. Either way, help.

choices.text: I know a guy who might have one.
Let me go to the server room.

vr-demo-fix.speaker: vendor-representative
vr-demo-fix.text: You have a USB? Is it clean? Has it seen action? ...Okay, let's do this. Plug it in and I'll hold my breath.

choices.text: Here goes nothing.

vr-demo-success.speaker: vendor-representative
vr-demo-success.text: IT'S ALIVE! The demo is running! You just saved my Q4 targets. Take this coupon — it's for a limited edition AI Toaster-shaped speaker.

choices.text: Thanks! I'll treasure it forever.

sg-greeting.speaker: security-guard
sg-greeting.text: Good evening. I'm Officer Dan. This is my conference. Please move along unless you have pressing business.

choices.text: I'm looking for the VIP Rooftop Lounge.
Just lost, sorry.

sg-vip.speaker: security-guard
sg-vip.text: That keycard is from our previous event. The account's been disabled... but between us? I'll cut you a break. Head on up.

choices.text: Thanks, Dan. I won't tell anyone.

sf-greeting.speaker: startup-founder
sf-greeting.text: Hey! Nice beard. Real or conference beard? Just kidding, that's my icebreaker. I'm Priya from QuantumByte. We're disrupting the disruption space. You seem like you know things.

choices.text: What's QuantumByte do?
Disrupt me harder, baby.

sf-what.speaker: startup-founder
sf-what.text: We make AI-powered stress balls for devs. Series A funded, 12 employees, a ping-pong table, and our CTO is a very good golden retriever named Pixel.

choices.text: I'm sold. What do you need?
This sounds insane.

sf-need.speaker: startup-founder
sf-need.text: I need someone who can handle pressure — and by pressure I mean a broken demo, a crashing kiosk, and angry investors. If you help out at least two people around here, I want to make you an offer.

choices.text: I'm already busy with quests.

co-greeting.speaker: conference-organizer
co-greeting.text: Oh no. Another attendee. I'm Derek, Conference Organizer. I have not slept in 36 hours and the keynote speaker just bailed on us.

choices.text: I can help! What do you need?
That's rough. Have you tried coffee?

co-help.speaker: conference-organizer
co-help.text: Ideally? A new speaker. But since this conference has 3,000 attendees and a keynote in 18 hours, I'll take miracles. Also, the speaker's USB went missing.

choices.text: I can deliver the keynote.
Let me investigate the missing USB.

co-coffee.speaker: conference-organizer
co-coffee.text: I've consumed enough caffeine to reanimate a Victorian child. It's not coffee. It's structural integrity. But jokes aside, you seem helpful.

choices.text: I'm listening. What happened?

co-interested.speaker: conference-organizer
co-interested.text: You HAVE to be kidding. You know what, at this point I'll take anyone. But you need a Speaker Pass. The only way to get one is if... hmm, actually nobody's ever asked me this before.

choices.text: Where can I get a Speaker Pass?
Forget it. I'll find the USB.

co-pass.speaker: conference-organizer
co-pass.text: I issue them. But I'm not handing one out to just anyone — that's how I got in this mess in the first place. Do at least two good deeds here and prove you're not a flake.

choices.text: Challenge accepted.

co-usb.speaker: conference-organizer
co-usb.text: Actually, the Speaker Pass might also unlock access to areas that could help. Visit me once you've recovered the data. And check the casino — our courier was last seen there.

choices.text: Will do. Let's find this USB.

inf-greeting.speaker: influencer
inf-greeting.text: Oh my GOD. Are you a REAL developer or are you just here for the content? Follow for follow? No? Okay, rude.

choices.text: I'm a real developer. I fix things.
Can I at least get a photo?

inf-real.speaker: influencer
inf-real.text: Prove it. I saw a vendor demo crash earlier. If you fix it, I'll give you a shoutout to my 2.4M followers. In tech, that's basically fame.

choices.text: I'll look into it.
My code doesn't beg for validation.

inf-photo.speaker: influencer
inf-photo.text: Ugh. Fine. But I'm not doing the duck face. I have standards. ...There. Post it with #DevConnectVibes and tag me.

choices.text: Perfect. Now I'm famous.

it-greeting.speaker: it-technician
it-greeting.text: Zane, IT. If your laptop is making a noise like a dying bird, I'm not fixing it. But I'm always happy to help people who remember my name from previous years.

choices.text: The Vendor's demo USB is corrupted. Can you make a new one?
Can you tell me about the server room?
Do you have a spare USB drive?

it-usb.speaker: it-technician
it-usb.text: Ah, CloudNine's kiosk again. Classic. I've got fresh deployment media right here. Grab one and don't wipe it, I have family photos on it too.

choices.text: Thanks! Hope the photos are safe.

it-usb-has.speaker: it-technician
it-usb-has.text: Looks like you already have one. Smart.

choices.text: Foresight is my middle name.

it-server.speaker: it-technician
it-server.text: It's just through the Theater. Speaker Pass only. Don't go in there and start rebooting things. Last conference, an attendee 'accidentally' brought down the entire hotel for three hours.

choices.text: Three hours? Now THAT'S impressive.
I'll be careful.

it-server-impressed.speaker: it-technician
it-server-impressed.text: I know, right? To this day no one knows what he did. He just kept pressing buttons and laughing. True legend.

choices.text: A man of culture.

it-spare.speaker: it-technician
it-spare.text: Of course. I'm a walking USB drive at this point. Here. Don't install anything weird on it.

choices.text: Promises, promises.

bt-greeting.speaker: bartender
bt-greeting.text: Welcome to the CloudNine Rooftop! I'm Marcus. We're doing a conference special: 'The Error 500' — it's tequila, regret, and something I found in the back.

choices.text: I'll take one.
Do you know anything about a VIP event here?
Just water for me.

bt-drink.speaker: bartender
bt-drink.text: Coming right up. That'll be $18. Or, alternatively, you could tell me your worst conference story and I'll comp it. Conference solidarity, you know?

choices.text: My worst story...
Keep the change. Here's $25.
On second thought, water is fine.

bt-story.speaker: bartender
bt-story.text: Perfect. While you drink, let me tell you about the time I forgot my own machine's password live on stage. Three thousand people. Camera flash. The sound of a thousand pity-claps.

choices.text: You're an inspiration, Marcus.

bt-vip.speaker: bartender
bt-vip.text: VIP event? Upstairs. Elevator is unlocked — just tap the keycard. I don't think they've changed the codes since last year. Don't tell Security.

choices.text: Noted. Thanks, Marcus.

ks-greeting.speaker: keynote-speaker
ks-greeting.text: Oh. You found me. Look, it's not what you think. I'm not bailing. I'm... exploring alternative engagement formats.

choices.text: Your slides went missing. What do you know?
You're a keynote speaker. You have obligations.

ks-slides.speaker: keynote-speaker
ks-slides.text: The USB? I handed it to a courier before I left. Then I came here and... actually, can we just say I had an emergency? Because I did. Existentially.

choices.text: Give me the courier's info.
You owe them a talk.

ks-obligations.speaker: keynote-speaker
ks-obligations.text: You're right. I'm sorry. But honestly? This talk is terrible. I wrote it in 45 minutes in a hotel lobby using predictive text. You should give it.

choices.text: I'll give it IF I can get the slides back.
No way. You're giving the talk.

ks-courier.speaker: keynote-speaker
ks-courier.text: Her name is Chloe. She works VIP concierge. She said she'd be near the Casino floor. Tell her I'm sorry. Actually, no. Tell her I said the slides are at the bar.

choices.text: Alright, I'll find her.

ks-owes.speaker: keynote-speaker
ks-owes.text: Just... take the slides. I don't care anymore. Just don't make me look worse than I already have. The USB is at the bar counter. I'll tell Security you're a guest.

choices.text: Deal. You owe me.
